steps: Contact your Ironclad Customer Success Manager to have API access enabled on your account
Once API access is enabled, log in to your Ironclad instance at https://ironcladapp.com
Click on your profile icon in the top-right corner to open the dropdown menu
Click 'Generate New Token' or 'Create API Token'
Give the token a descriptive name and configure the required scopes
Copy the generated token immediately — it will not be shown again
Use the token as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of API requests

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Token
credential_fields.description: Your Ironclad Bearer token generated from the API settings page

notes: API access requires an Enterprise Ironclad plan; contact your CSM to enable it
The token is shown only once at creation — store it securely immediately
Ironclad supports both API token (Bearer) and OAuth 2.0 (client credentials / authorization code) flows
API tokens do not expire but can be revoked from the settings page
All API requests must use HTTPS
